factors.summary: The company experienced both favorable and unfavorable impacts from pricing actions. Favorable price and mix contributed positively, but higher commodity and freight costs pressured margins.

evidence.text: Gross profit margins in the first six months of 2019 decreased 90 bps to 28.2% compared to the first six months of 2018. Our profit margin decreased 80 bps from higher commodity costs, 110 bps from higher freight and distribution costs, 30 bps from tariffs on Chinese imports and 90 bps from other product costs. These were partially offset by 130 bps from favorable price and mix combined, 60 bps from sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions and 30 bps from the divested Australia, New Zealand, Asia, Brazil and Kysor Warren businesses which had lower margins.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through June 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through June 30, 2018 - Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faced unfavorable impacts from higher costs despite positive contributions from price and mix.

factors.summary: Revenue mix shifted towards lower-margin segments due to divestitures and unfavorable factors, negatively impacting margins.

evidence.text: Gross profit margins in the first six months of 2019 decreased 90 bps to 28.2% compared to the first six months of 2018. Our profit margin decreased 80 bps from higher commodity costs, 110 bps from higher freight and distribution costs, 30 bps from tariffs on Chinese imports and 90 bps from other product costs. These were partially offset by 130 bps from favorable price and mix combined, 60 bps from sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions and 30 bps from the divested Australia, New Zealand, Asia, Brazil and Kysor Warren businesses which had lower margins.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through June 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through June 30, 2018 - Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The revenue mix shifted towards lower-margin segments, negatively impacting margins.

factors.summary: Sales volume declined by 3%, partially offset by price and mix improvements.

evidence.text: Net sales decreased 6% in the first six months of 2019 compared to the first six months of 2018 due to a 5% decline related to the sale of our Australia, New Zealand, Asia and Brazil and Kysor Warren businesses, a 3% decline in sales volume and 1% from unfavorable foreign currency exchange rates. These were partially offset by 3% from favorable price and mix combined.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through June 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through June 30, 2018 - Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Sales volume declined, though partially offset by price and mix improvements.

factors.summary: The Refrigeration segment experienced a significant decline due to divestitures and unfavorable foreign exchange, while Residential and Commercial segments saw mixed performance.

evidence.text: Refrigeration net sales decreased 27% in the first six months of 2019 compared to the first six months of 2018. The loss of sales from our divested Australia, New Zealand, Asia and Brazil and Kysor Warren businesses contributed 26% and unfavorable foreign currency exchange rates contributed 2%, partially offset by 1% favorable price and mix combined.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through June 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through June 30, 2018 - Results by Segment

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The Refrigeration segment saw a significant decline, while other segments had mixed performance.

factors.summary: Management confidence is cautious, as indicated by reduced share repurchases and increased net borrowings, suggesting a focus on liquidity and capital preservation.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by financing activities in the first six months of 2019 was $97 million compared to net cash used of $62 million in the first six months of 2018. The increase in cash provided by financing activities is largely due to a reduction in the amount of share repurchases in 2019 compared to 2018 and higher net borrowings on our revolving credit facility. During the first six months of 2019, we repurchased $250 million of shares as compared to $350 million of shares in the first six months of 2018.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management reduced share repurchases and increased borrowings, indicating caution.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include higher input costs, tariffs, and unfavorable foreign exchange rates, which are pressuring margins and sales.

evidence.text: Gross profit margins in the first six months of 2019 decreased 90 bps to 28.2% compared to the first six months of 2018. Our profit margin decreased 80 bps from higher commodity costs, 110 bps from higher freight and distribution costs, 30 bps from tariffs on Chinese imports and 90 bps from other product costs. These were partially offset by 130 bps from favorable price and mix combined, 60 bps from sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions and 30 bps from the divested Australia, New Zealand, Asia, Brazil and Kysor Warren businesses which had lower margins.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through June 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through June 30, 2018 - Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include higher costs, tariffs, and foreign exchange pressures.

factors.summary: CapEx is expected to increase, particularly due to reconstruction efforts and investments in systems and software, signaling confidence in future operational improvements.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures were $54 million in the first six months of 2019 compared to $43 million in the first six months of 2018. Capital expenditures in 2019 were primarily related to the reconstruction of the Marshalltown facility as well as equipment and investments in systems and software to support the overall enterprise.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased CapEx suggests confidence in future operational improvements.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is slightly increasing, with the debt-to-total-capital ratio rising to 117.4% from 116.8%, indicating moderate deleveraging efforts.

evidence.text: Our debt-to-total-capital ratio increased to 117.4% at June 30, 2019 from 116.8% at December 31, 2018.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Debt leverage slightly increased, but the change is minimal and could indicate moderate deleveraging efforts.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are being managed cautiously, with reduced share repurchases and continued dividend payments, indicating a balance between capital preservation and returning value.

evidence.text: During the first six months of 2019, we repurchased $250 million of shares as compared to $350 million of shares in the first six months of 2018. We also returned $51 million to shareholders through dividend payments in the first six months of 2019.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company is balancing capital preservation with returning value through dividends while reducing share repurchases.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation has deteriorated, with net cash used in operating activities increasing significantly compared to the prior year.

evidence.text: The net cash used in operating activities in the first six months of 2019 and 2018 reflects the seasonal increase in working capital requirements. The increase in cash used in operating activities in the first six months of 2019 as compared to 2018 was primarily due to the timing of working capital payments.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow has deteriorated with increased cash used in operating activities.

These were partially offset by 130 bps from favorable price and mix combined, 60 bps from sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions and 30 bps from the divested Australia, New Zealand, Asia, Brazil and Kysor Warren businesses which had lower margins.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Trade tariffs are causing significant margin pressure through higher costs on imported components.

factors.summary: Litigation exposure remains a concern, particularly related to asbestos and environmental liabilities, though the company has provisions in place to manage these risks.

evidence.text: The net change in unrealized losses on unsettled futures contracts was due to changes in commodity prices relative to the unsettled futures contract prices. For more information on our futures contracts, see Note 5 in the Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements. For more information on special legal contingency charges and asbestos-related litigation, see Note 7 in the Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements. The environmental liabilities relate to estimated remediation costs for contamination at some of our facilities.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through June 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through June 30, 2018 - Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Litigation exposure is a concern, particularly regarding asbestos and environmental liabilities, though provisions are in place.

factors.summary: Interest rate increases are pressuring the company's financial position, with interest expense rising due to higher rates and increased borrowings.

evidence.text: Interest expense, net of $24 million in the first six months of 2019 increased from $18 million in the first six months of 2018 due to an increase in our average net borrowings and higher interest rates.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through June 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through June 30, 2018 - Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher interest rates are increasing interest expense, pressuring the company's financial position.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are unfavorably impacting results, contributing to both revenue and margin pressures.

evidence.text: Net sales decreased 6% in the first six months of 2019 compared to the first six months of 2018 due to a 5% decline related to the sale of our Australia, New Zealand, Asia and Brazil and Kysor Warren businesses, a 3% decline in sales volume and 1% from unfavorable foreign currency exchange rates. These were partially offset by 3% from favorable price and mix combined.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through June 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through June 30, 2018 - Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements are causing revenue and margin pressures.

factors.summary: Demand trends show mixed performance, with seasonal weather and economic conditions impacting demand. While there are slight declines in net sales and volume, these are partially offset by price and mix improvements.

evidence.text: The demand for our products and services is seasonal and significantly impacted by the weather. Warmer than normal summer temperatures generate demand for replacement air conditioning and refrigeration products and services, and colder than normal winter temperatures have a similar effect on heating products and services. Conversely, cooler than normal summers and warmer than normal winters depress the demand for HVACR products and services.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

evidence.text: Net sales decreased 6% in the first six months of 2019 compared to the first six months of 2018 due to a 5% decline related to the sale of our Australia, New Zealand, Asia and Brazil and Kysor Warren businesses, a 3% decline in sales volume and 1% from unfavorable foreign currency exchange rates. These were partially offset by 3% from favorable price and mix combined.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through June 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through June 30, 2018 - Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends are mixed, with some declines offset by price and mix improvements.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a deceleration in organic revenue growth, which is attributed to both unfavorable weather conditions and divestitures. These factors have led to a decrease in net sales and segment profits, particularly in key segments like Residential Heating & Cooling.

evidence.text: Results for the second quarter of 2019, most notably in our Residential Heating & Cooling segment, were impacted by unfavorable weather conditions in North America when compared to the second quarter of 2018. The Residential Heating & Cooling segment saw a 4% decrease in net sales primarily on unfavorable weather and their segment profit was flat, including the impact of insurance proceeds received for lost profits.
evidence.subsection: Financial Overview

evidence.text: Net sales decreased 6% in the first six months of 2019 compared to the first six months of 2018 due to a 5% decline related to the sale of our Australia, New Zealand, Asia and Brazil and Kysor Warren businesses, a 3% decline in sales volume and 1% from unfavorable foreign currency exchange rates. These were partially offset by 3% from favorable price and mix combined.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through June 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through June 30, 2018 - Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth has decelerated due to unfavorable weather and divestitures, leading to decreased net sales and segment profits.

factors.summary: Input costs are increasing, driven by higher commodity and freight expenses, as well as tariffs, which are putting pressure on profit margins. The company is attempting to mitigate these cost increases through various strategies such as price adjustments, commodity contracts, futures and fixed forward contracts, along with cost reduction initiatives.

evidence.text: Raw material costs are volatile, primarily due to commodity price fluctuations and tariffs, but the company is taking measures to mitigate these impacts.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

evidence.text: Gross profit margins in the first six months of 2019 decreased 90 bps to 28.2% compared to the first six months of 2018. Our profit margin decreased 80 bps from higher commodity costs, 110 bps from higher freight and distribution costs, 30 bps from tariffs on Chinese imports and 90 bps from other product costs. These were partially offset by 130 bps from favorable price and mix combined, 60 bps from sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions and 30 bps from the divested Australia, New Zealand, Asia, Brazil and Kysor Warren businesses which had lower margins.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through June 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through June 30, 2018 - Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are rising significantly, driven by higher commodity prices, freight expenses, and tariffs, which are putting substantial pressure on profit margins.

factors.summary: The company's gross profit margins have been under pressure, showing a negative trajectory due to higher commodity prices, freight costs, and tariffs. Despite efforts to mitigate these impacts through favorable price and mix adjustments, sourcing cost reductions, and divestitures, the overall margins have compressed. The negative factors outweigh the positive adjustments, leading to a net decline in both quarterly and year-to-date margins.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin in the second quarter of 2019 decreased to 30.2% compared to 30.8% in the second quarter of 2018. We saw decreases in profit margin of 60 basis points ('bps') from higher commodity prices, 100 bps from higher freight and distribution costs, 30 bps from tariffs on Chinese imports and 90 bps from other product costs. Partially offsetting these decreases were positive margin increases of 100 bps from favorable price and mix, 30 bps from favorable product warranty costs, 20 bps from sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions and 70 bps from the divested Australia, New Zealand, Asia, Brazil and Kysor Warren businesses which had lower margins.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of 2019 Compared to Second Quarter of 2018 - Consolidated Results

evidence.text: Gross profit margins in the first six months of 2019 decreased 90 bps to 28.2% compared to the first six months of 2018. factors.summary: Key sensitivities include commodity prices, tariffs, and foreign exchange rates, which have significant impacts on margins and sales.

evidence.text: The demand for our products and services is seasonal and significantly impacted by the weather. Warmer than normal summer temperatures generate demand for replacement air conditioning and refrigeration products and services, and colder than normal winter temperatures have a similar effect on heating products and services. Conversely, cooler than normal summers and warmer than normal winters depress the demand for HVACR products and services. In addition to weather, demand for our products and services is influenced by national and regional economic and demographic factors, such as interest rates, the availability of financing, regional population and employment trends, new construction, general economic conditions, and consumer spending habits and confidence. The principal raw materials used in our manufacturing processes are steel, copper and aluminum. In recent years, pricing volatility for these commodities and related components, including the impact of newly imposed tariffs on the import of certain of our raw materials and components, has impacted us and the HVACR industry in general.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

evidence.text: Gross profit margins in the first six months of 2019 decreased 90 bps to 28.2% compared to the first six months of 2018. Our profit margin decreased 80 bps from higher commodity costs, 110 bps from higher freight and distribution costs, 30 bps from tariffs on Chinese imports and 90 bps from other product costs. These were partially offset by 130 bps from favorable price and mix combined, 60 bps from sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions and 30 bps from the divested Australia, New Zealand, Asia, Brazil and Kysor Warren businesses which had lower margins.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through June 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through June 30, 2018 - Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is highly sensitive to commodity prices, tariffs, and exchange rates, which pose significant risks to margins and sales.
